description: Hummingbird size and shape consistent with Archilocus. Bright green back and crown. Face with blackish lores and dark green auriculars, contrasting with bright white, unmarked, throat. Underparts whitish with olive sides and just a tiny bit of buff (visible from only some angles) on rear flanks by legs. Bill all black, shorter than that of BCHU in direct comparison. Important tail features not visible except in photos. Plumage looked moderately worn, consistent with aging as an adult. Useful photos, incorporated into this report, were taken when the bird was dusted off the feeder by a BCHU. When feeding, the bird did not spread, nor wag, its tail at all. At one time, feeding for a goodly 30 seconds uninterrupted.
